prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "俯卧 W 字" (prone W raise) exercise, bird's-eye view from directly above looking down at the floor. Person posture: Athletic person LYING FACE-DOWN ON A YOGA MAT ON THE FLOOR (PRONE position) — chest, abdomen, hips, and front of thighs pressed flat into the mat. Forehead resting on the mat, head down. Legs extended straight together. Both arms bent at the elbows: shoulders abducted out to the sides at about 90 degrees from the body axis, elbows bent so the forearms come back toward the head with hands near the ears, thumbs pointing toward the ceiling. From a top-down view this forms the LETTER W (the head and arms together making a W shape). The arms are LIFTED A FEW CENTIMETERS off the mat by retracting and depressing the shoulder blades. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: this person is LYING DOWN ON THE FLOOR FACE-DOWN. The person is NOT STANDING. The person is NOT UPRIGHT. The body is HORIZONTAL ON THE GROUND. Show the full body lying flat from a top-down perspective.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: lower trapezius and external rotators of the shoulders (infraspinatus, teres minor). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: rhomboids and rear deltoid. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "下斜方肌" pointing to the lower trapezius; "肩外旋肌群" pointing to the external rotators of the shoulders; "菱形肌 / 三角肌后束" pointing to the rhomboids and rear deltoid. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
